# synix

> A build system for agent memory with declarative pipelines, content-addressed caching, and full provenance tracking

## What it does

Synix transforms raw conversation data (ChatGPT exports, Claude exports, text files) into searchable, hierarchical memory artifacts through configurable LLM-powered transform pipelines. Change a pipeline configuration and only affected layers rebuild automatically. Think `make` or `dbt`, but for AI agent memory with full lineage tracking from any artifact back to source conversations.

## Key concepts

- **Artifact** — immutable, versioned build output (transcript, episode, rollup, core memory) with SHA256 content addressing
- **Layer** — typed pipeline stage (Source for inputs, Transform subclasses for LLM steps, SearchIndex/FlatFile for projections)  
- **Pipeline** — declared in Python with `Pipeline.add(*layers)` routing transforms to layers and projections automatically
- **Projection** — materializes artifacts into usable outputs (SearchIndex with SQLite FTS5 + embeddings, FlatFile markdown docs)
- **Provenance** — every artifact traces back to source conversations, always included in search results
- **Cache/Rebuild** — fingerprint-based comparison of inputs, prompts, and transform code determines incremental rebuilds

## Installation and quick start

```bash
# Install and initialize
uvx synix init my-project
cd my-project

# Add API key, then build
uvx synix build

# Query and explore
uvx synix list                    # all artifacts by layer
uvx synix search "hiking"         # full-text search with provenance
uvx synix show final-report       # render specific artifact
```

## CLI commands

- `synix build` — run pipeline, materialize projections, only rebuild changed artifacts
- `synix plan` — dry-run showing rebuild decisions with cost estimates and cache explanations  
- `synix search <query>` — search with provenance chains, supports `--layers` and `--mode hybrid`
- `synix list [layer]` — show all artifacts, optionally filtered by layer
- `synix show <id>` — display artifact content, resolves by label or ID prefix
- `synix lineage <id>` — full provenance tree from artifact to sources
- `synix validate` — run validators against build artifacts (experimental)
- `synix clean` — delete build directory
- `synix init <name>` — scaffold new project with sources, pipeline, and README

## Architecture overview

```
src/synix/
├── __init__.py            # Public API: Pipeline, Source, Transform, SearchIndex, FlatFile, Artifact
├── core/
│   └── models.py          # Layer hierarchy (Source, Transform, SearchIndex, FlatFile, Pipeline)
├── build/
│   ├── runner.py          # Execute pipeline — walk DAG, run transforms, cache artifacts
│   ├── plan.py            # Dry-run planner — per-artifact rebuild/cached decisions
│   ├── dag.py             # DAG resolution — build order from depends_on references
│   ├── artifacts.py       # Artifact storage — save/load/query (filesystem-backed)
│   ├── provenance.py      # Provenance tracking — record and query lineage chains
│   ├── fingerprint.py     # Build fingerprints — synix:transform:v2 scheme
│   ├── llm_transforms.py  # Bundled memory transforms + shared LLM helper functions
│   ├── transforms.py      # Transform base + registry (string dispatch fallback)
│   ├── validators.py      # Built-in validators (PII, SemanticConflict, Citation, etc.)
│   ├── fixers.py          # Built-in fixers (SemanticEnrichment, CitationEnrichment)
│   └── projections.py     # Projection dispatch
├── transforms/
│   └── base.py            # Generic platform transforms: MapSynthesis, GroupSynthesis, ReduceSynthesis, FoldSynthesis
├── search/
│   ├── indexer.py         # SQLite FTS5 — build, query, shadow swap
│   ├── embeddings.py      # Embedding provider — fastembed, OpenAI, cached
│   └── retriever.py       # Hybrid search — keyword + semantic + RRF fusion
└── cli/                   # Click CLI commands
```

## Pipeline definition

Pipelines use Python objects, not strings. Dependencies are expressed as object references:

```python
from synix import Pipeline, Source, SearchIndex
from synix.transforms import MapSynthesis, ReduceSynthesis

pipeline = Pipeline("my-pipeline")
pipeline.source_dir = "./sources"
pipeline.llm_config = {"provider": "anthropic", "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"}

bios = Source("bios", dir="./sources/bios")
work_styles = MapSynthesis("work_styles", depends_on=[bios], 
    prompt="Infer work style:\n\n{artifact}", artifact_type="work_style")
report = ReduceSynthesis("report", depends_on=[work_styles],
    prompt="Team analysis:\n\n{artifacts}", label="team-report")

pipeline.add(bios, work_styles, report)
pipeline.add(SearchIndex("search", sources=[work_styles, report], search=["fulltext"]))
```

## Important constraints

- SQLite only for storage (no external databases required)
- Python 3.11+ required
- Filesystem-based artifact storage with content addressing
- No web UI (CLI-focused tool)
- LLM providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, or OpenAI-compatible endpoints via base_url
- Single-machine builds (distributed mesh mode experimental)
